title: festuca stricta subsp. saxatilis
intro: Festuca stricta subsp. saxatilis, also known as sheep's fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antispasmodic to ease muscle cramps. Festuca stricta subsp. saxatilis is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
